continuity.arc_goal: Build unified Health SQLite database (health.db) with cross-pillar queries: podcast health knowledge + blood lab history + Oura Ring biometrics. Then build health_query.py read layer so the agent can answer 'how has my ApoB changed and what does Attia say about it' meaningfully via iMessage/BlueBubbles.

progress_update.summary: We completed the full health-db feature lifecycle today: scope → plan → execute → clean → test → evaluate → sweep. Along the way we also addressed the top 3 architecture issues flagged by the Opus review (module location, lab upsert correctness, unit tracking), captured 3 SPECTRE skills, disabled Telegram in favour of BlueBubbles/iMessage, and restarted the agent. Everything is pushed and the working tree is clean. Next session starts health_query.py — the read layer that makes the DB actually useful.
progress_update.goal: Build health.db — unified SQLite DB with 3 data pillars + a read API (health_query.py) that lets the agent answer cross-pillar health questions via iMessage/BlueBubbles.
accomplished: Addressed top 3 architecture issues from Opus review: moved health_db.py to workspace/health/, fixed lab_results upsert (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE preserving audit trail), added canonical_unit to lab_markers with auto-population from marker names (96/117 populated)
Ran /spectre:clean: removed 5 dead-code items (find_by_show, _check_fts5, stale help string, dead nan guards, redundant FTS rebuild)
Ran /spectre:test: 83 behavioral tests across 4 files, all passing — test_health_db, test_health_store, test_health_store_cmd, test_import_blood_labs

Ran /spectre:evaluate: Opus architecture review saved to docs/tasks/main/reviews/architecture_review_health_db.md; 3 SPECTRE skills saved (decisions-health-knowledge-architecture v2, integration-oura-v2-api, patterns-sqlite-fts5-python)
Ran /spectre:sweep + /spectre:rebase: all committed and pushed to origin/main (bf8646f)
Disabled Telegram channel (BlueBubbles is now sole active channel); agent restarted
progress_update.now: Session complete. Everything pushed, agent running. Ready to start health_query.py in next session.
next_steps: Run /spectre:scope to scope health_query.py — the read layer (lab_trend, search_health_knowledge, daily_brief, oura_window functions + SKILL.md intent for iMessage queries)
Decide in scope: add events table (interventions/correlations) and out-of-range flagging in this pass or defer
Implement health_query.py as an exec-able script the agent calls from SKILL.md
Remaining architecture issues from review: migration runner (A2), backup strategy (A3), daily Oura sync (O1) — defer unless user wants them
constraints: health_db.py now lives at agents/sample-agent/workspace/health/health_db.py (moved from podcast-summary/scripts/)
health.db now lives at agents/sample-agent/workspace/health/health.db (moved from podcast_vault/)
BlueBubbles is sole active channel — Telegram disabled but config preserved
Agent runs on Freedom (M5 Max); BlueBubbles troutbot user must stay logged in via Fast User Switching
Planning docs live at ~/Code Projects/Health DB/ (not in ironclaw repo)
health_store.py public API (append_entry, load_all, find_by_show) must be preserved — engine.py and on_demand.py cannot change
DB accessible from both host (python3.13) and container (python3)
All host scripts use /usr/local/bin/python3.13; deps installed via pip3.13
No jq in container — all JSON handling in skill scripts must use python3
FTS5 verified available in both host and container Python
Oura heartrate API only returns recent data — historical intraday HR not available via v2 API
Oura API gap: Jan 1 – Jun 15 2016 not available via API (app shows it, API does not)
Blood lab Excel at ~/Documents/Fitness/BloodTest_Tracker.xlsx — re-run import script if file is updated
Weekly Oura sync runs Monday 3am via launchd; token in agents/sample-agent/.env
decisions: health_query.py should be exec-able from SKILL.md — same pattern as on_demand.py
Telegram disabled (not deleted) — re-enable anytime by setting enabled: true in openclaw.json
health_query.py scope session is the next action — don't implement without scoping first
Option A (swap-in place): health_store.py internals replaced with SQLite, public API unchanged — zero changes to callers
launchd oura sync uses StartCalendarInterval (batch/weekly) not KeepAlive (daemon) — different from whisper-server pattern
Blood lab import runs --dry-run first to handle unknown Excel layout quirks before committing
Jeff has Gen3 Oura ring — pull all endpoints including stress, resilience, VO2 max; handle 404s gracefully
Oura heartrate 'missing' data is an API limitation — not fixable, accepted as-is
daily_spo2 is the correct Oura v2 endpoint (not spo2); daily_hrv endpoint doesn't exist (HRV via sleep sessions avg_hrv)
All planning docs now live in ~/Code Projects/{Project Name}/ — not inside the ironclaw repo
health_db.py moved to workspace/health/ after Opus review flagged location
open_questions: Should health_query.py scope include events table + out-of-range flagging, or keep v1 minimal (just query functions)?
What does a health answer look like in an iMessage reply? (UX question for scope session)
risks: Remaining architecture issues (no migration runner, no backup, weekly vs daily Oura sync) — low urgency but worth addressing before adding more pillars

context.session_arc: This is the completion of the health-db epic that started on 2026-04-26. Session 1 scoped and planned. Session 2 implemented Phases 1-3 (foundation, blood labs, Oura). Session 3 wrote tests (4 files). Session 4 ran clean/test/evaluate, fixed architecture issues, pushed everything, and disabled Telegram. Total elapsed: all on 2026-04-26.
